title: Excess Credits Request
content:
Excess Credits Request
Morgan State University - Office of the Registrar

This guide is for STUDENTS. It explains how to request permission to take more than the standard credit limit in a given semester.

---

STEP 1: ENTER YOUR NAME
Enter your full name.

---

STEP 2: ENTER ACADEMIC INFORMATION
Enter your major, classification, GPA, and probation status. You can find this information on your unofficial transcript, in Degreeworks, or on your student profile.

---

STEP 3: ENTER TERM AND COURSE INFORMATION

Regular Class Load Limits:
- Good standing: 18 credits maximum
- Engineering majors: 19 credits maximum
- Students on probation: 15 credits maximum

Select the semester: Fall, Winter, Spring, or Summer.
Enter the total number of credits you plan to take.
List the classes you plan to register for.

---

STEP 4: ACKNOWLEDGE REGISTRATION DEADLINE
Your excess credits request must be approved before the add/drop deadline. Deadlines are published on the Academic Calendar. Any requests still in progress by the deadline will be automatically voided.

---

STEP 5: SUBMIT
Submit the form. Processing takes up to 10 business days. Both the Chairperson and Dean must approve the request.

For the full official guide with screenshots, view: [Excess Credits Guide](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oDsY_32JM-xAJFClpCnnvHGILVBSMoHm/view)
